company_metadata.company_name: Salem Stainless Steel Suppliers Private Limited

database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Sale of Goods maps 1:1 to CMA Domestic Sales. No export sales for this entity.

Previous year phrasing — FY22-23 used 'Revenue from operations (gross)' (Note 19) vs FY23-24 onwards 'Sale of products' / 'Sale Of Goods & Services' (Note 14). Auditor changed from Neerag Jain to A.C. Jain.

database_entries.industry_note: In trading companies, CMA requires bifurcation of purchases into Imported and Indigenous. This split is not available in the consolidated FS notes — it comes from internal import records.
database_entries.reasoning: Split — Imported portion of total purchases. For FY22-23, imported was zero (all indigenous). For FY23-24 = 3.51 Cr, FY24-25 = 6.03 Cr.

database_entries.industry_note: In trading companies, trading rebates and discounts received are sometimes netted against purchases in CMA rather than shown as Other Income. FY22-23: ~6.34 Cr netted, FY23-24: ~8.29 Cr netted, FY24-25: zero netting.
database_entries.reasoning: Split — Indigenous portion of purchases. Also absorbs netting of trading income in FY22-23 and FY23-24.

database_entries.industry_note: In trading companies, ALL depreciation is placed in Manufacturing Cost (Row 12) with ZERO in Admin/SGA. In manufacturing companies, depreciation is typically split between Manufacturing (Row 12) and Admin (within SGA Row 23) based on asset class.
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Total depreciation from Dep Schedule (Note 7) = Rs 9,610,568 = 0.961 Cr. The CA put 100% in manufacturing since the assets (warehouses, vehicles, equipment) are all used for trading operations.

database_entries.industry_note: In trading companies, Power & Fuel relates to warehouse/godown operations and is classified as manufacturing expense. In manufacturing, many more items (consumables, stores, factory rent, etc.) go here.
database_entries.reasoning: Split — Power & Fuel extracted from Other Expenses Note 20 and placed in manufacturing. Verified: Rs 5,894,743 = 0.589 Cr matches CMA Row 13.
database_entries.validation_issue: FY22-23 Other Mfg Exp (5.07 Cr) is much larger than FY23-24 (0.61 Cr) and FY24-25 (0.59 Cr). The FY22-23 detailed schedules likely included freight inward and material testing charges in manufacturing. The exact FY22-23 breakdown requires reading the 697-row Conso-sch P&L schedule granularity.

database_entries.industry_note: In trading companies, there is no WIP or Raw Material inventory — only Finished Goods (stock-in-trade). Opening stock = previous year's closing BS Inventory. CMA Opening FG (Row 19) = BS Inventories as at previous year-end.
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Opening FG for FY24-25 = Closing Inventory from FY23-24 BS = Rs 767,498,197 = 76.75 Cr. Note: this is from prior year BS Note 9.

Direct match — Closing FG = current year BS Inventories (Note 9) = Rs 776,354,037 = 77.64 Cr. Cross-check: Changes in stock (Note 17) = Opening - Closing = 76.75 - 77.64 = -0.89 Cr = P&L figure of -Rs 8,855,840.

database_entries.industry_note: In trading companies, ALL employee expenses go to SGA (Row 23). In manufacturing companies, employee cost is typically split between manufacturing wages (Row 45) and admin salary (within SGA).
database_entries.reasoning: Component 1 of 12 for SGA — Entire Employee Benefits goes to SGA. No manufacturing workforce in a trading company.
Component 2 of 12 for SGA — Audit fees are always SGA. | [CA 2026-04-15] Updated from R71->R73: Auditor's Remuneration -> R73 per CA_VERIFIED_2026 rule 16
Component 3 of 12 for SGA — Advertising and sales promotion. | [CA 2026-04-15] Updated from R71->R70: Advertisement and Sales Promotion -> R70 per CA rule 23
Component 4 of 12 for SGA — Travel and communication expenses.
Component 5 of 12 for SGA — Subscriptions and membership fees.
Component 6 of 12 for SGA — Insurance charges.
Component 7 of 12 for SGA — Legal and professional fees. Note: original text has misspelling 'Proffessional' preserved as-is.
Component 8 of 12 for SGA — Office and general expenses.
Component 9 of 12 for SGA — Catch-all 'Other Expenses' bucket.

database_entries.industry_note: In manufacturing companies, Rent is typically split between Factory Rent (manufacturing) and Office Rent (SGA). In trading companies, all rent goes to SGA since there are no factory premises.
database_entries.reasoning: Component 10 of 12 for SGA — Rent and taxes. All to SGA since this is a trading company with no factory.
Component 11 of 12 for SGA — Repairs and maintenance.

database_entries.cma_field_name: Interest on Fixed Loans / Term loans
database_entries.industry_note: CMA requires splitting total interest into Term Loan interest (Row 27) and Working Capital interest (Row 28). The FS often shows only a single 'Finance Costs' line. The CA uses bank-wise interest certificates to bifurcate.
database_entries.reasoning: Split — Term loan interest portion of total finance costs. Verified: Row 27 + Row 28 = 2.39 + 7.17 = 9.56 Cr = P&L Finance Costs.

Split — Working capital interest portion of total finance costs. OD interest on HDFC and HSBC accounts. | [CA 2026-04-15] Updated from R84->R83: Finance Costs -> R83 per CA rule 17 (bare label; breakdown in notes)

database_entries.industry_note: For FY24-25, P&L Other Income (1.96 Cr) maps directly. But for FY22-23 and FY23-24, the CA NETTED trading rebates/interest from Other Income against Purchases. FY22-23: P&L Other Income=8.32 Cr but CMA Row 30=1.98 Cr (6.34 Cr netted). FY23-24: P&L=10.61 Cr, CMA=2.32 Cr (8.29 Cr netted). This is common for trading companies where rebates effectively reduce cost of goods.
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match for FY24-25. For prior years, this is a SPLIT from P&L Other Income after netting trading income against purchases.

database_entries.industry_note: Trading company pattern: CA nets trading income against cost of goods. The FS Other Income of 10.61 Cr includes trading rebates that effectively reduce purchase cost, so only 2.32 Cr is truly non-operating.
database_entries.reasoning: Split — Non-operating portion of Other Income. The trading rebates (8.29 Cr) were netted against CMA Raw Materials instead.

database_entries.industry_note: Same netting pattern as FY23-24. Different note reference due to auditor change.
database_entries.reasoning: Split — Non-operating portion of FY22-23 Other Income after netting trading rebates.

database_entries.raw_text: Loss on disposal of fixed assets (estimated)
database_entries.reasoning: Small amount (Rs 56,666) appearing only in FY24-25. Likely loss on disposal of motor vehicle: Dep Schedule shows vehicle deletion (Gross 17,81,652 - Dep Write-back 17,18,978 = NBV 62,674). The loss = NBV minus sale proceeds. Exact source not confirmed in FS notes. Marked as unmatched — manually verify from sale deed or vehicle transfer records. | [CA 2026-04-15] Updated from R93->R89: Loss on disposal of fixed assets -> R89 per CA_VERIFIED_2026 rule 2
database_entries.validation_issue: Amount Rs 56,666 does not exactly match any single FS line item. Best estimate is loss on vehicle disposal (NBV Rs 62,674 minus estimated sale proceeds of Rs 6,008).

database_entries.reasoning: CMA Provision for Taxes = Current Tax (1.9262) + Previous Tax (0.0641) + Deferred Tax (-0.0228) = 1.9675 Cr. This is the NET total tax expense from P&L. Decomposed in CMA Rows 35-37.
Direct match — Current year income tax provision from P&L = Rs 19,261,703 = 1.926 Cr. Excludes previous year tax adjustments and deferred tax.

CMA DTL (Row 36) = P&L 'Previous tax' (Rs 641,254 = 0.0641 Cr) + P&L 'Deferred tax' (Rs -228,087 = -0.0228 Cr) = 0.0413 Cr. The CA combined prior-year tax adjustments with deferred tax into a single 'DTL' line. Only non-zero in FY24-25.
database_entries.validation_issue: This combines two distinct P&L items (Previous tax and Deferred tax) into CMA 'DTL'. The label is slightly misleading since it includes prior-year cash tax, not just deferred.

database_entries.reasoning: FY22-23: DTA = 0.062 Cr (P&L deferred tax was -6.20 L = net DTA creation). FY23-24: DTA = 0.019 Cr. FY24-25: DTA = 0 (the deferred tax was positive/combined with prior tax in Row 36 instead). | [CA 2026-04-15] Updated from R101->R100: (e) Deferred tax / 'Deferred Tax' P&L charge -> R100 per CA_VERIFIED_2026 rule 12
Component 1 of 3 — HDFC OD balance Rs 130,538,890 = 13.054 Cr. Part of CMA ST Bank Borrowings which also includes HSBC OD and CMLTD.
Component 2 of 3 — HSBC OD balance Rs 267,547,906 = 26.755 Cr.

database_entries.industry_note: CMA standard requires Current Maturities of Long-term Debt (CMLTD) to be shown under Current Liabilities, even though the BS may show the full term loan balance under Non-current Liabilities. The CA reclassified ~9.12 Cr of term loan instalments due within 1 year from LT Borrowings (Note 2) to CMA Row 101.
database_entries.reasoning: Component 3 of 3 — CMLTD reclassified from Note 2 Long-term Borrowings. This explains why CMA Row 101 (48.93 Cr) > BS Short-term Borrowings (39.81 Cr). The difference of 9.12 Cr = current portion of bank term loans. Only applicable in FY24-25; FY22-23 and FY23-24 had CMA Row 101 = BS ST Borrowings exactly. | [CA 2026-04-15] Updated from R131->R136: Current Maturities of Long-term Debt -> R136 per CA_OVERRIDE O3
database_entries.validation_issue: CMLTD amount (9.12 Cr) is derived as CMA Row 101 minus BS Note 3 total. Cannot be independently verified from the FS notes which don't show current/non-current bifurcation of term loans. For FY22-23 and FY23-24, no CMLTD reclassification was needed (CMA = BS exactly).

database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Note 4(a) Sundry Creditors for purchases = Rs 40,898,050 = 4.090 Cr. The CA split Trade Payables into goods creditors (Row 106) and expense creditors (Row 112.3).
Only non-zero in FY22-23 (2.01 Cr). Sourced from FY22-23 BS Note 6 'Other Current Liabilities' which included customer advances. In FY23-24 and FY24-25, this is zero.

FY24-25: Zero. FY23-24: 1.83 Cr = current tax provision (Rs 18,271,126). FY22-23: 1.44 Cr from BS Note 7 (Short-term Provisions). The CMA shows tax provision in CL when there's an outstanding liability; in FY24-25 the tax was either paid or netted against advance tax.
FY23-24: Current tax provision carried on BS = Rs 18,271,126 = 1.83 Cr.

Direct match — Note 6(a) Statutory Dues = Rs 29,785,496 = 2.979 Cr. Includes GST payable, TDS payable, PF/ESI dues, and other statutory liabilities. FY23-24: 4.48 Cr (Note 6a), but CMA Row 110 = 2.65 Cr — the difference (1.83 Cr) went to Row 108 as income tax provision.
database_entries.validation_issue: For FY23-24, Note 6(a) Statutory Dues = Rs 44,776,504 = 4.48 Cr but CMA Row 110 = 2.65 Cr. The CA split statutory dues into income tax provision (Row 108 = 1.83 Cr) and other statutory (Row 110 = 2.65 Cr).

database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Note 4(b) Sundry Creditors for expenses = Rs 25,623,437 = 2.562 Cr. Zero in FY22-23 (all trade payables went to Row 106).
Component 1 of 2 — Note 5 Other Current Liabilities = Rs 417,481 = 0.042 Cr.
Component 2 of 2 — Note 6(b) Other Provisions = Rs 20,977,308 = 2.098 Cr. Combined with Note 5 Other CL (0.042 Cr) to give Row 112.4 total of 2.139 Cr.

database_entries.raw_text: Kotak Bank A/c - Car Loan + Kotak Bank Term Loan + HDFC Bank Term Loan + HSBC Bank Term Loan
database_entries.industry_note: CMA 'Term Loans' (Row 117) contains only SECURED bank term loans, MINUS CMLTD reclassified to CL. Unsecured related-party loan goes to 'Other Term Liabilities' (Row 119). For FY24-25, the large HDFC TL (8.75 Cr) is mostly CMLTD, leaving only 2.62 Cr in Row 117.
database_entries.reasoning: Secured bank term loans from Note 2 minus CMLTD. FY24-25: Kotak Car (0.281) + Kotak TL (1.325) + HDFC TL portion + HSBC TL portion = 2.623 Cr after CMLTD reclassification. FY23-24: 5.63 Cr = Kotak Car (0.455) + HDFC TL (2.770) + HSBC TL (2.035) + Axis (0.373) = 5.633 Cr exactly.
database_entries.validation_issue: FY24-25 CMLTD allocation across individual term loans is not disclosed in FS. The aggregate secured TL in Note 2 = 11.38 Cr, but CMA Row 117 = 2.62 Cr, implying 8.75 Cr CMLTD. Exact loan-wise CMLTD split requires repayment schedules not available in FS.

database_entries.industry_note: Unsecured loans from related parties (promoters, group entities) are often partially treated as Quasi Equity in CMA analysis. Banks consider a fixed portion (~Rs 6 Cr here) as subordinated/quasi-equity for NWC computation, improving the debt-equity ratio. Four Star Estates LLP is a related party (associated entity).
database_entries.reasoning: Split — Four Star Estates LLP unsecured loan minus Quasi Equity. FY22-23: 57.71 - 6.0 = 51.71 Cr. FY23-24: 38.21 - 6.0 = 32.21 Cr. FY24-25: 35.68 - 6.0 = 29.68 Cr.
Direct match — Share Capital = Rs 23,960,000 = 2.396 Cr. 23,96,000 shares of Rs 10 each. Unchanged across all three years.

database_entries.cma_field_name: Balance transferred from profit and loss a/c
database_entries.section: Reserves and Surplus — Surplus of Profit & loss Account
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Note 1A(B) P&L Surplus closing balance = Rs 555,345,802 = 55.53 Cr. Also includes 'SHRI GANESH MAHARAJ' entry of Rs 1.25 (negligible). Cross-check: Opening 49.87 + PAT 5.66 = 55.53 ✓.

database_entries.cma_field_name: Balance transferred from profit and loss a/c
database_entries.reasoning: Split — P&L surplus portion extracted from total Reserves. FY22-23 Note 2 = 64.77 Cr = P&L Balance (45.01) + Share Premium (19.75) + Shri Ganesh Maharaj (~0).
Direct match — Note 1A(A) Share Premium = Rs 197,540,000 = 19.754 Cr. Unchanged across all three years.

database_entries.industry_note: Quasi Equity treatment is a CMA convention (not in the FS). Banks require unsecured related-party loans to be bifurcated: a 'hard core' portion as quasi equity improves apparent net worth and NWC ratios. The Rs 6 Cr is fixed by bank agreement, not a percentage.
database_entries.reasoning: Split — Rs 6 Cr from Four Star Estates LLP loan moved to Net Worth as Quasi Equity. Constant across all 3 years. Remaining loan balance goes to Row 119 (Other TL).
Direct match — Note 11(a) Cash Balances = Rs 4,521,800 = 0.452 Cr.

Direct match — Note 11(b) Bank Balances = Rs -3,116,462 (negative due to OD overdraw) + adjustments. CMA shows 0.056 Cr. Note: FY24-25 Bank Balance in Note 11 is actually negative (-Rs 3,116,462) but CMA shows positive 0.056 Cr — the CA likely netted with some other bank balance or used absolute value.
database_entries.validation_issue: Note 11(b) shows negative bank balance (-Rs 3,116,462 = -0.312 Cr) but CMA Row 202 shows positive 0.056 Cr. The CA may have used a different bank balance figure or reclassified the overdraft.

database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Note 11(c) Fixed Deposits = Rs 52,067,315 = 5.207 Cr. Classified as short-term investments in CMA since these are bank FDs with <1 year maturity.

database_entries.cma_field_name: Domestic Receivables ( including bills purchased and discounted)
database_entries.reasoning: Split — Current trade receivables (< 6 months) from Note 10. FY24-25: Rs 569,568,913 = 56.96 Cr.

database_entries.industry_note: In trading companies, all inventory is 'Stock-in-trade' (finished goods) — no Raw Materials or WIP inventory. CMA Row 213 (Finished Goods) = entire BS Inventory. Rows 210 (Raw Materials), 211 (WIP), 212 (Stores & Spares) are all zero.
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Note 9 Stock in Trade = Rs 776,354,037 = 77.64 Cr. This is the only inventory category for a trading company.
Split — Advance tax portion from FY22-23 statutory balances. Only non-zero in FY22-23 (7.69 Cr).

Split — Prepaid portion from FY22-23 Other Current Assets. Only non-zero in FY22-23 (0.065 Cr). | [CA 2026-04-15] Updated from R222->R223: 'Other current assets' label matches R223 canonical 'Other Advances / current asset' — not R222 (Prepaid Expenses)
Component 1 of 3 — Statutory balances (GST credit, TDS, TCS, advance tax) = Rs 64,830,410 = 6.483 Cr.
Component 2 of 3 — Other advances (employee advances, vendor advances) = Rs 5,370,906 = 0.537 Cr.
Component 3 of 3 — Other Current Assets (Note 13) = Rs 7,612,803 = 0.761 Cr. Combined with ST Loans & Advances to form CMA Row 222.

database_entries.section: Property, Plant and Equipments — Gross Block
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Depreciation Schedule total Gross Block closing = Rs 170,695,509 = 17.070 Cr. Includes: Land (4.88), Building (2.98), P&M (5.11), Vehicles (2.73), Office Equip (0.56), Computer (0.53), Furniture (0.26), Others (0.01).
Direct match — Depreciation Schedule total Accumulated Depreciation closing = Rs 75,512,244 = 7.551 Cr. Cross-check: Net Block = Gross (17.070) - Acc Dep (7.551) = 9.518 Cr = BS PPE (Rs 95,183,265).

database_entries.raw_text: (a) Outstanding for Period Above Six Month, Unsecured, considered good
database_entries.industry_note: CMA classifies receivables > 6 months as non-current assets (excluded from NWC computation). This reduces the assessed bank finance requirement. Banks scrutinize this for ageing quality.
database_entries.reasoning: Split — Overdue trade receivables from Note 10(a) = Rs 115,872,393 = 11.587 Cr. Cross-check: Row 207 (56.96) + Row 232 (11.59) = 68.54 ≈ Note 10 total (68.54) ✓.
Direct match — Note 8 Share Investments = Rs 73,956,030 = 7.396 Cr. These are long-term equity investments in group/associate entities.

FY22-23 only — Long-term Loans & Advances = 60.26 Lakhs = 0.603 Cr (deposits, security deposits, rental advances). In FY23-24 and FY24-25, the BS shows zero for LT Loans & Advances and Other Non-current Assets.

database_entries.industry_note: The CA mapped Deferred Tax Asset (DTA) to the CMA 'Intangible Assets' row (Row 241). This is a creative placement — DTA is not truly an intangible asset, but the CMA form has limited rows for non-current assets, and DTA doesn't fit neatly elsewhere. FY22-23: 0.061 Cr, FY23-24: 0.080 Cr, FY24-25: 0.103 Cr. All match BS DTA exactly.
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — BS Deferred Tax Assets (Net) = Rs 1,028,660 = 0.103 Cr mapped to CMA 'Intangible Assets'. This is a non-standard placement by the CA since CMA doesn't have a dedicated DTA row.
database_entries.validation_issue: CMA label says 'Intangible Assets' but the source is Deferred Tax Asset, not a traditional intangible (software, goodwill, etc.). This company has no actual intangible assets.
